title: Have Your Say On Proposed Brown Crab Conservation Measures in the South West
body: "This follows strong support from the South West Crab Trial Group for harmonising the Minimum Conservation Reference Size (MCRS) for brown crab. The proposed MCRS would be set at 160 millimetres (mm) for males and 150 mm for females.  With stakeholder support, and anticipated long-term benefits to crab stocks, the government intends to implement the proposed MCRS increase as a permanent measure, rather than a trial. This is subject to further stakeholder input.   The survey is now closed  MMO opted for a short, mobile-friendly online survey to make it easier for stakeholders to participate and share their views. The survey was open until 23:59 on 17 August 2025.  Thank you to everyone who participated and helped share the survey within their networks.  MMO will publish the results shortly.  "
